commitMessage: Use static methods from declaring classes.
categoryReason: The commit involves internal API changes and code reorganization within Eclipse JDT components, which is characteristic of internal refactoring.
dslRule:
<!id: jdt.internal.refactoring.static-member-move>
<!description: Consolidates static methods and constants from various JDT UI classes into common utility classes (e.g., MembersOrderPreferenceCache to MembersOrderPreferenceCacheCommon, ToStringGenerationSettings to ToStringGenerationSettingsCore). Improves internal API consistency and organization.>
<!severity: info>
<!minJavaVersion: 8>
<!tags: jdt, internal, refactoring>

@id: jdt.internal.membersview.is-member-order-property.to-common
org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.preferences.MembersOrderPreferenceCache.isMemberOrderProperty($eventProperty)
=> org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.manipulation.MembersOrderPreferenceCacheCommon.isMemberOrderProperty($eventProperty)
;;

@id: jdt.internal.tostringdialog.settings.template-names.to-core
org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.codemanipulation.tostringgeneration.ToStringGenerationSettings.SETTINGS_TEMPLATE_NAMES
=> org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.codemanipulation.tostringgeneration.ToStringGenerationSettingsCore.SETTINGS_TEMPLATE_NAMES
;;

@id: jdt.internal.tostringdialog.settings.templates.to-core
org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.codemanipulation.tostringgeneration.ToStringGenerationSettings.SETTINGS_TEMPLATES
=> org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.codemanipulation.tostringgeneration.ToStringGenerationSettingsCore.SETTINGS_TEMPLATES
;;

@id: jdt.internal.tostringdialog.settings.selected-template.to-core
org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.codemanipulation.tostringgeneration.ToStringGenerationSettings.SETTINGS_SELECTED_TEMPLATE
=> org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.codemanipulation.tostringgeneration.ToStringGenerationSettingsCore.SETTINGS_SELECTED_TEMPLATE
;;
summary: This commit refactors internal JDT UI code by replacing references to static methods and constants from older classes with their counterparts in newer, dedicated common utility classes. Specifically, `MembersOrderPreferenceCache.isMemberOrderProperty()` is moved to `MembersOrderPreferenceCacheCommon.isMemberOrderProperty()`, and several static preference keys from `ToStringGenerationSettings` (e.g., `SETTINGS_TEMPLATE_NAMES`) are moved to `ToStringGenerationSettingsCore`. These are direct 1:1 replacements of fully qualified static member accesses, improving code organization and internal API consistency. The transformations are fully implementable as multiple GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Move tryWithResourceCleanUp into jdt.core.manipulation

Signed-off-by: Jessica He <jhe@redhat.com>
irrelevantReason: This commit represents an architectural refactoring: moving the implementation of an existing cleanup from 'org.eclipse.jdt.ui' to 'org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation'. It also abstracts the detailed AST manipulation logic, replacing it with a delegation to a core fix. The DSL is designed for defining transformation patterns, not for describing internal code movement or refactoring of a cleanup's internal implementation details.
summary: Architectural refactoring of an existing cleanup's implementation and internal delegation, not a new transformation pattern expressible by the DSL.

commitMessage:
Add new preference to ignore inexact refs for Code Minings (#397)

- add a new preference to ignore inexact results for Code Mining
  references and have this used instead of the Search preference
- fixes #370
categoryReason: The rule modernizes internal Eclipse JDT UI API usage by replacing an old preference lookup mechanism with a new, specific one, aligning with internal API evolution.
summary: This commit replaces an internal Eclipse Search UI preference lookup with a dedicated JDT UI preference check for Code Minings. This is a direct 1:1 method call replacement, improving internal API consistency and decoupling. It is fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule, applicable from Java 8 baseline.

commitMessage:
Add support for inline return tags (#340)

* Add support for inline return tags
irrelevantReason: This commit introduces new NLS message keys, adds a new private helper method (findTags), and modifies existing control flow (replacing a loop with new logic, adding new if statements, and extending boolean conditions). These are structural changes (adding/removing methods, adding if blocks, replacing loop bodies with new statements) or additions of new string constants, which are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL that focuses on expression and annotation replacements.
categoryReason: The changes are structural and specific to JDT's internal Javadoc rendering, not fitting into typical transformation categories.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs support for adding new methods, defining new local variables, replacing entire statement blocks (like loops or if-else if chains), and adding new if statements. The pattern of adding an OR condition (e.g., '|| isReturn') to an existing boolean expression might be supported in isolation, but it's part of larger structural changes here.
summary: This commit adds support for inline Javadoc return tags by introducing new NLS entries, a new recursive helper method (`findTags`), and refactoring existing Javadoc rendering logic to incorporate the new tag element. The changes are highly structural, involving the creation of new lists, method calls, and conditional logic to parse and display the return tags. These architectural and control-flow modifications are not expressible as pattern-based transformations in the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Make quickfix that suggests importing a class smarter (#352)

* Make quickfix that suggests importing a class smarter

- change UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor.addSimilarTypeProposals() to
  check if an interface is expected in which case make sure that
  each suggested class to import has the interface in its hierarchy
- fixes #349
categoryReason: The change enhances an existing JDT quick-fix's internal logic for filtering type proposals by performing complex AST and binding resolution to check inheritance hierarchies. This is an internal architectural enhancement to JDT's intelligence, not a pattern that transforms user code directly.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need extensive capabilities to: 1. Dynamically create an ASTParser and set its project. 2. Resolve bindings for arbitrary IJavaElements. 3. Perform recursive type hierarchy checks (like the new isInherited method). 4. Filter or generate quick-fix proposals based on complex semantic analysis, which goes beyond simple pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit enhances an internal JDT quick-fix that suggests importing classes. It adds complex logic to `UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor.addSimilarTypeProposals()` to ensure that suggested classes to import implement or extend an expected interface. This involves creating an `ASTParser`, resolving bindings for `IJavaElement`s, and performing a recursive inheritance check (via a new `isInherited` helper method). This is an internal architectural enhancement for quick-fix filtering, requiring sophisticated AST and binding analysis. The current TriggerPattern DSL is designed for pattern-based code transformations, not for implementing complex, dynamic filtering logic with AST manipulation and binding resolution. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Make Text Block indentation consistent (#429)

- make text block indentation the same whether the open quotes
  occur on a separate line or at the end of another line
- fixes #414
categoryReason: The change modifies an internal JDT UI component's algorithm for calculating text block indentation based on contextual line information and partition types. This is an internal algorithmic change within JDT's formatter/editor infrastructure, not a pattern to transform user-written code.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support modifying complex algorithms, adding new 'if' / 'else if' blocks with intricate conditions based on text content and partition types, or performing detailed line-by-line text analysis. These capabilities are fundamental to implementing formatter logic but are beyond the scope of expression-level or simple statement-level pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit refactors JDT's `IndentAction` to make text block indentation consistent. It introduces new conditional logic and complex calculations within the `computeIndent()` method to determine the correct indentation string for text blocks, considering whether the opening quotes appear on a separate line or are inline. This is a highly specific internal algorithmic change to JDT's formatter and editor. The TriggerPattern DSL is not capable of expressing such complex control flow modifications, heuristic-based text analysis, or algorithmic updates. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Remove code referencins Navigator view

The notice for removal is since 2019
categoryReason: The commit's primary intent is the complete removal of a deprecated UI component (Navigator view) and its associated API from the codebase. This involves not only removing API calls but also deleting static field declarations and entire source files, which are structural changes beyond the current DSL's capabilities for pattern-based transformations. There is no direct replacement API; the feature is simply gone.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs capabilities to safely delete field declarations (e.g., `public static final String ID_RES_NAV = ...;`) and to flag or manage the deletion of entire source files.
summary: The commit removes all references to the deprecated Eclipse Navigator view, including deleting specific method calls, removing a static field declaration (`JavaPlugin.ID_RES_NAV`), and deleting an entire action class (`ShowInNavigatorViewAction.java`). While removing method calls (e.g., `layout.addShowViewShortcut(...)`) could be expressed as a pattern-to-empty-replacement rule (`pattern => ;;`), the deletion of field declarations and entire source files are structural changes that the current TriggerPattern DSL cannot directly implement. Therefore, the commit is classified as RED, as a comprehensive automation of this change is not possible with the current DSL capabilities.

commitMessage:
[20] Allow JDT to target Java 20 in compiler preferences (#463)

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/426
irrelevantReason: This commit primarily adds support for Java 20 in internal Eclipse JDT compiler preferences. It involves declaring new constants, adding them to internal arrays of supported versions, and updating conditional logic for displaying preview feature warnings in a UI configuration block. These are specific internal configuration and feature enablement changes within the JDT UI codebase, not generalizable code transformation patterns applicable to user code or even widely reusable across other Eclipse plugins. The DSL is designed for structural code transformations, not for updating internal configuration arrays or conditional display logic based on Java versions.
summary: This commit enables JDT to target Java 20 in compiler preferences by updating internal version constants, arrays listing supported versions, and conditional logic for preview feature warnings within the `ComplianceConfigurationBlock` UI class. These are highly specific internal configuration changes and not generalizable code transformation patterns suitable for the TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Add additional checking for convert if to conditional (#447)

- don't convert if boxing is involved but types of if/else are
  different
- fixes #446
categoryReason: This change refines the behavior of an existing quick-fix to prevent incorrect transformations under specific type conditions.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support adding complex conditional blocks (if statements) with custom type-binding analysis (resolveBoxing, isEqualTo) that can prevent the application of a transformation. Current DSL guards operate on the matched pattern, not on the properties of the *resulting* expressions after a hypothetical rewrite.
summary: This commit adds an `if` block with complex type binding analysis to an existing JDT quick-assist (`AdvancedQuickAssistProcessor`) to prevent incorrect conversions from `if-else` to conditional expressions when boxing is involved and types differ. This structural modification with advanced semantic checks (`resolveBoxing`, `isEqualTo`) is beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL's capabilities, classifying it as RED.
dslValidationResult: Parse error at line 1: Rule starting at line 1 is missing ';;' terminator (line 1)

commitMessage:
Move StringFix to StringFixCore in jdt.core.manipulation (#449)

- fixes #448
categoryReason: This is an internal architectural refactoring within the Eclipse JDT codebase to separate core logic into a manipulation bundle and adapt calling code to the new structure.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need capabilities for: (1) Class/package renaming and updating all references across multiple files, (2) Structural code insertion including `return new Wrapper(coreFix)` with a ternary expression, and (3) Adding explicit type casts to method arguments (e.g., `(ProblemLocation)problem`) based on internal API changes.
summary: This commit performs an internal architectural refactoring of JDT's `StringCleanUp`. It moves core logic from `StringFix` to `StringFixCore`, updates imports, modifies method calls, introduces an `ICleanUpFixCore` wrapper, and adds explicit casts to arguments. These changes involve class/package renaming, complex structural code insertion (for the wrapper and ternary return), and argument casting, which are not directly expressible in the current TriggerPattern DSL. Hence, it is classified as RED.

summary: This commit moves a static utility method `createChainCode` from `ChainCompletionTemplateBuilder` to `ChainElement`, updating its call sites. It improves modularity by placing utility logic in a more appropriate internal JDT Core manipulation class. The rule explicitly replaces the fully qualified call to the old method with the new fully qualified one.

It does not contain any code transformation patterns applicable to user code. It involves adding NLS entries and registering a new preference checkbox. These changes are part of the internal JDT UI configuration and do not represent a direct code transformation pattern for user code, thus not applicable for DSL mining.

commitMessage:

Merge from R4_27_maintenance branch to BETA_JAVA20 branch

The remaining changes (in `IndentAction.java` and `UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor.java`) are complex structural additions of new logic (text block indentation, type inheritance checks with new helper methods and imports). These types of changes (deletion of files/constants, complex structural code additions, and functional logic implementations) are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations.

These changes are either wholesale deletions or involve intricate structural modifications and additions of new functional blocks, which are not amenable to simple pattern-based DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Make Open Type dialog icons consistent with Outline View (#479)

- add logic to properly calculate the effective modifiers for
  classes and interfaces that are nested in interfaces
- fixes Bug 567663
categoryReason: The change is an internal bug fix within Eclipse JDT's UI component, specifically refactoring how type modifiers are calculated and retrieved to ensure correct display of icons for nested types. This falls under internal JDT API modernization and bug fixing.
summary: This commit fixes Bug 567663 by modifying how type modifiers are obtained in `TypeNameMatchLabelProvider.java`. It replaces a direct call to `typeRef.getModifiers()` with a call to a newly introduced private static helper method, `getEffectiveModifiers(typeRef.getType(), typeRef.getModifiers())`. This ensures correct calculation of modifiers for nested classes/interfaces, affecting their icons in the Open Type dialog. The transformation is a direct 1:1 expression replacement (a method call for another method call), which is fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. The rule uses an `inClass` guard for precision, targeting an internal JDT UI code fix. The helper method itself is assumed to exist in the target codebase, as this is an internal refactoring.

commitMessage:
Fix regression in UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor (#486)

* Fix regression in UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor

- fix problem introduced by #352
- check for inheritance should not be run if the suggested
  class binding has same name as the missing class
- fixes #485
categoryReason: The commit specifically addresses an internal regression in JDT's UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor.
languageChangeNeeded: The commit introduces a new 'if' statement that wraps an existing block of code. The current TriggerPattern DSL does not support structural transformations like inserting new control flow statements (e.g., 'if' blocks) around existing code. While one part of the change involves modifying a boolean expression, the primary fix is structural.
summary: This commit addresses a regression in `UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor`. It involves two main changes: 1) Wrapping an existing block of code with a new `if (javaElementType != null)` statement. This is a structural change (inserting a new control flow block) that is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based replacements. 2) Modifying an existing boolean expression within an `if` condition to include an additional check (`!qualifiedTypeBinding.getName().equals(simpleBinding.getName())`). While this second part is a direct expression replacement and would be implementable as a DSL rule, the first part constitutes a significant structural modification. As a result, the entire commit is classified as RED due to the DSL's limitation in inserting new statement-level control flow.

irrelevantReason: The commit primarily involves commenting out obsolete Java code that displayed a 'BETA' disclaimer, removing multi-line documentation disclaimers from Java source and properties files, and changing a UI label in a .properties file (from '20 (BETA)' to '20'). None of these changes represent a reusable Java code transformation pattern suitable for the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on transforming active Java source code elements.
summary: This commit removes obsolete beta disclaimers and updates a UI label in a .properties file. These changes are not code transformations that can be generalized into a TriggerPattern DSL rule for Java source code.

commitMessage:
Merge branch 'BETA_JAVA20'

Conflicts:
	org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF
	org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation/pom.xml
	org.eclipse.jdt.ui/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF
	org.eclipse.jdt.ui/pom.xml
categoryReason: Updates an internal configuration related to supported Java versions in Eclipse JDT UI.
summary: This commit, a merge of 'BETA_JAVA20', primarily updates internal Eclipse JDT UI preferences to support Java 20. The most direct and generalizable pattern is the update of a conditional expression from checking for `JavaCore.VERSION_19` to `JavaCore.VERSION_20`. This is a direct 1:1 constant replacement within a boolean expression, suitable for a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. Other changes involving array literal modifications and NLS message updates are not directly expressible with simple DSL rules. The rule will be added to `jdt-internal-refactoring.sandbox-hint` for internal JDT API modernization. No specific `sourceVersionGE` guard is needed in the rule as it's an internal JDT refactoring for JLS version support, not a migration of user code to a new Java language feature, thus baseline Java 8 is sufficient.

commitMessage:
Merge pull request #496 from iloveeclipse/master

Merge BETA_JAVA20 into master
categoryReason: Updates an internal configuration related to supported Java versions in Eclipse JDT UI.
summary: This commit is a merge request that is identical in its diff to Commit 0, updating internal Eclipse JDT UI preferences to support Java 20. The same core pattern of replacing a conditional check from `JavaCore.VERSION_19` to `JavaCore.VERSION_20` is present. Therefore, this commit is a duplicate of Commit 0 and is classified as GREEN with the same DSL rule. It will be added to `jdt-internal-refactoring.sandbox-hint`.

commitMessage:
Don't use deprecated SecurityManager, use StackWalker instead

Have to fix this warning to fix compilation error in Jenkins

See https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/493
categoryReason: Modernizing usage of a deprecated API (SecurityManager) to a modern alternative (StackWalker).
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently does not support deleting/adding entire classes, or transforming complex method bodies involving new API usage (StackWalker) and custom helper classes/lambda predicates. A significant DSL extension would be needed to handle such structural transformations.
summary: This commit replaces the usage of the deprecated `SecurityManager.getClassContext()` for stack frame inspection with `java.lang.StackWalker`. The transformation involves removing an entire helper class (`AccessChecker`), introducing a new helper class (`OutlineAccessChecker`) with a `StackWalker` instance and lambda predicates, and refactoring the `isCalledByOutline()` method to use this new helper. Due to the deletion and addition of entire classes, and the complex restructuring of method bodies with new Java 9+ API (`StackWalker`) and lambda expressions, this transformation is highly structural and beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. It cannot be expressed as a simple pattern-based rule.

commitMessage:
move change signature utils to manipulation (#428)

Signed-off-by: Shi Chen <chenshi@microsoft.com>
categoryReason: The commit performs an internal refactoring within Eclipse JDT UI to centralize constants, moving them to a new core utility class.
summary: This commit refactors the internal `JavaTypeCompletionProcessor` class by moving hardcoded string constants and their derived expressions to static fields in a new `JavaTypeCompletionProcessorCore` class. This improves maintainability and centralizes constant definitions within JDT's internal architecture. Two distinct, direct 1:1 expression replacements are identified: one replacing a string literal with a static field, and another replacing an infix string concatenation with a static field. Both are fully implementable as GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules, enhancing internal JDT API usage. The rules explicitly use fully qualified names for precision in patterns and replacements.

languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for removing conditional blocks and complex array literal replacements based on context.
summary: This commit removes the logic for copying file paths to the clipboard via `FileTransfer` and `IPath.toOSString()` unless explicitly requested. This involves removing a conditional block and simplifying array initializers for `data` and `dataTypes`. This is a structural change to internal JDT UI behavior that is too complex for the current TriggerPattern DSL, requiring removal of conditional blocks and conditional array literal simplification.

commitMessage:
Fix additional problems caused by #352 (#514)

* Fix additional problems caused by #352

- don't do inheritance check for parameterized, generic, record, wildcard,
  captured, raw, or recovered types
- fixes #434
- add tests to QuickFixTest1d8
categoryReason: This is an internal refactoring within Eclipse JDT UI to refine type inheritance checks based on modern Java language features.
summary: This commit extends a boolean conditional expression in `UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor.java` to prevent inheritance checks for specific `ITypeBinding` kinds, such as generic, parameterized, wildcard, raw, record, and recovered types. This is a direct 1:1 replacement of a boolean expression, enhancing the correctness of internal JDT quick-fix logic for modern Java features. The transformation is fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule, guarded by `sourceVersionGE(16)` due to the `isRecord()` check.

commitMessage:
Don't add closing quote to three quotes if text blocks are supported (#515)

- fixes #509
categoryReason: Relates to the behavior of the Java editor (auto-closing quotes, text block support).
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need capabilities to introduce new local variable declarations (e.g., 'boolean textBlockSupported = JavaModelUtil.is15OrHigher(project);') and then reference these newly introduced variables in subsequent expression modifications. Currently, the DSL primarily supports pattern matching and replacement of existing expressions, statements, or annotations, not the insertion of new, interdependent declarations and their immediate use in altered control flow conditions. Additionally, the existing part of the if condition `next != null && next.length() > 1 || previous != null && previous.length() > 1` cannot be easily isolated for replacement without matching the entire surrounding context, which is prone to over-matching. If the entire `if` condition were to be replaced, it would be a very large and brittle pattern.
summary: This commit modifies the Java editor's auto-closing quote logic to prevent incorrect behavior when text blocks are supported (Java 15+). The change involves introducing a new local variable to check Java version compatibility (`textBlockSupported = JavaModelUtil.is15OrHigher(project)`) and then extending an existing boolean condition within a 'switch' statement to use this new variable. The DSL cannot directly implement this, as it requires injecting a new local variable declaration and then referencing it in a modified expression within the same transformation context. This is a complex structural change that goes beyond simple expression or statement replacement.

commitMessage:

categoryReason: The change fixes a potential IndexOutOfBoundsException, addressing a correctness issue.
summary: This commit fixes an `IndexOutOfBoundsException` by extending an `if` condition to include a bounds check (`indexOfSelElement < rootCPListElement.getChildren().size()`). This is a direct 1:1 boolean expression replacement, fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule, improving code correctness. The rule is guarded to target the specific internal JDT method.

commitMessage:
Fix New Java Project Wizard layout (#529)

* Fix New Java Project Wizard layout

- fix layout to accommodate long JRE names
- fixes #517
categoryReason: The change involves modifying UI layout parameters in an Eclipse wizard, which falls under UI configuration.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support complex, multi-statement replacements that involve declaring new local variables, setting properties on them, and then using those variables in subsequent expressions, especially when replacing an existing single expression with a sequence of operations on a newly introduced variable. For example, replacing '$var.setLayoutData(new GridData(...))' with a block like 'GridData gd = new GridData(...); gd.horizontalSpan = 2; $var.setLayoutData(gd);' is not currently possible with the expression-oriented DSL.
summary: This commit fixes a layout issue in the New Java Project Wizard by adjusting `GridLayout` columns, `doFillIntoGrid` arguments, and explicitly setting `GridData.horizontalSpan`. While simple literal argument changes are directly implementable, the `GridData` transformations require replacing a single method call argument with a sequence of statements (local variable declaration, assignment to its property, and then using the new variable). This complex structural change is currently beyond the capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL's expression-based rewrite, classifying it as RED.

commitMessage:
Fix SIOOBE in IndentAction when moving start of text block (#534)

- fixes #572
- don't calculate additional indentation for start of text block
  and don't apply text block indent rules to start of text block
  if previous token is a comma which implies an array init or
  a parameter list for example (i.e. use default indentation rules
  for array init or parameters)
categoryReason: The commit fixes a bug within an internal Eclipse JDT UI component (IndentAction), not a general Java code pattern. This type of fix often involves intricate structural changes to internal logic, making it distinct from generic 'Bug Fix' or 'Probable Bugs' categories for user code.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support more complex structural transformations, such as inserting 'if' blocks, moving or wrapping existing code within new control flow statements, introducing new local variables, and handling multi-line conditional expressions that combine new and existing logic. The current DSL is primarily for expression-level or simple declaration-level replacements.
summary: This commit fixes a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException (SIOOBE) in JDT's `IndentAction` related to text block indentation. The changes involve introducing new local variables, adding complex conditional logic (`if (!prevLineEndsWithComma)`), and restructuring code flow. These structural modifications and complex expression rewrites are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on simpler, more direct pattern-to-replacement transformations.

commitMessage:
Don't filter type for method completion as type is known (#535)

* Don't filter type for method completion as type is known

- fixes #417
- add new test to MethodInsertCompletionTest
summary: This commit replaces an internal method call `isFiltered(proposal)` with `isIgnored(proposal.getKind())` within JDT's `CompletionProposalCollector`. This is a direct 1:1 method call replacement that updates the logic for filtering completion proposals. The transformation is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL, using an `inClass` guard to ensure its application to the specific internal component.

commitMessage:
Java Build Path : Adding an external jar in the Libraries tab
incorrectly changes the class path order in Order and Export tab #519

Class path order is fixed and can be changed manually on Order and
Export tab. Addition of any element to class path should be added at the
end of the list and previous elements should be at their location.

Fixed redundant notification to adapter in case of removing all elements
in the Libraries List.

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/519
categoryReason: Similar to Commit 0, this addresses a bug within internal Eclipse JDT UI components related to build path configuration. The nature of the changes (structural, new method declarations, complex control flow) is consistent with an internal bug fix rather than a generalizable code transformation.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support inserting 'if' blocks, changing field/method modifiers, converting local variables to fields, detecting and removing statements, and supporting complex structural changes in control flow. These capabilities are beyond the current DSL's scope.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the Java Build Path UI, addressing incorrect classpath order changes and redundant notifications. The changes involve adding null checks as new `if` blocks, modifying method and class visibility modifiers, converting local variables to fields, and introducing new helper methods. These are complex structural modifications and new declarations that cannot be expressed as pattern-based TriggerPattern DSL rules.
